---
title: "Research Activities"
format: html
---

::: {.lead}
Our group specializes in the continuum mechanics of soft, living, and active materials. By focusing on constitutive modeling, visco-hyperelasticity, and fluid-structure interactions, we tackle critical challenges in physiology and tissue engineering.
:::

::: {.d-flex .flex-wrap .gap-2 .mb-5 .mt-4}
<a href="#cornea" class="btn btn-outline-primary mb-2">👁️ Cornea Mechanics</a>
<a href="#eye" class="btn btn-outline-primary mb-2">👁️‍🗨️ Ocular Mechanics</a>
<a href="#clot" class="btn btn-outline-primary mb-2">🩸 Blood Clots</a>
<a href="#hydrogel" class="btn btn-outline-primary mb-2">🧫 Hydrogels & Biomaterials</a>
<a href="#others" class="btn btn-outline-primary mb-2">🔬 Soft Matter</a>
:::

---

<div id="cornea" style="padding-top: 2rem;"></div>
::: {.grid .align-items-center .mb-5}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](resources/images/members/Placeholder.png){.rounded .shadow-sm width="100%"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### Corneal Mechanics & Refractive Surgery

We investigate the intricate lamellar microstructure of the cornea to predict its response to surgical interventions (like LASIK or cross-linking) and degenerative diseases (such as keratoconus). Our approach combines inflation tests with microstructure-informed finite element models to characterize anisotropy and collagen remodeling.

**Key topics:** [Corneal anisotropy]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1} 
[Refractive surgery simulation]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1} 
[Collagen fiber remodeling]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1}

[📚 View Relevant Publications](publications.html?search=Cornea){.btn .btn-sm .btn-primary .mt-3}
:::
:::

<hr class="text-muted">

<div id="eye" style="padding-top: 2rem;"></div>
::: {.grid .align-items-center .mb-5 .mt-4}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](resources/images/members/Placeholder.png){.rounded .shadow-sm width="100%"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### Full Ocular Mechanics

Beyond the cornea, we model the mechanical environment of the entire eye globe. This includes analyzing the biomechanical factors contributing to glaucoma (optic nerve head deformation) and vitreoretinal interaction, treating the eye as a complex, pressurized multi-material system.

**Key topics:** [Glaucoma biomechanics]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1} 
[Optic nerve head deformation]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1} 
[Intraocular pressure modeling]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1}

[📚 View Relevant Publications](publications.html?search=Eye){.btn .btn-sm .btn-primary .mt-3}
:::
:::

<hr class="text-muted">

<div id="clot" style="padding-top: 2rem;"></div>
::: {.grid .align-items-center .mb-5 .mt-4}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](resources/images/members/Placeholder.png){.rounded .shadow-sm width="100%"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### Mechanics of Blood Clots & Hemostasis

Understanding the mechanics of thrombus formation and dissolution is crucial for tackling cardiovascular diseases. We characterize the viscoelastic and fracture properties of fibrin networks and blood clots under physiological flow conditions, aiming to predict clot stability, embolization risks, and the efficacy of thrombolytic therapies.

**Key topics:** [Thrombus visco-hyperelasticity]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1} 
[Fracture mechanics of fibrin]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1} 
[Fluid-structure interaction]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1}

[📚 View Relevant Publications](publications.html?search=Clot){.btn .btn-sm .btn-primary .mt-3}
:::
:::

<hr class="text-muted">

<div id="hydrogel" style="padding-top: 2rem;"></div>
::: {.grid .align-items-center .mb-5 .mt-4}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](resources/images/members/Placeholder.png){.rounded .shadow-sm width="100%"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### Synthetic Hydrogels & Biomimetic Materials

Hydrogels are essential tools for tissue engineering and mechanobiology. We study the nonlinear, time-dependent mechanics of synthetic and natural hydrogels to tailor their properties for specific biomedical applications, such as developing scaffolds that mimic the mechanical niche of Smooth Muscle Cells (SMCs).

**Key topics:** [Viscoelasticity of hydrogels]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1} 
[Porous media mechanics]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1} 
[Biomimetic scaffold design]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1}

[📚 View Relevant Publications](publications.html?search=Hydrogel){.btn .btn-sm .btn-primary .mt-3}
:::
:::

<hr class="text-muted">

<div id="others" style="padding-top: 2rem;"></div>
::: {.grid .align-items-center .mb-5 .mt-4}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](resources/images/members/Placeholder.png){.rounded .shadow-sm width="100%"}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
### Fundamental Soft Matter Mechanics & Others

We also apply our expertise to general problems in soft matter and active tissue mechanics, including cell-matrix interactions and the fundamental behavior of other soft biological tissues (vascular, skin, etc.) using novel experimental and numerical tools.

**Key topics:** [Smooth muscle cell mechanics]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1} 
[Tissue growth and atrophy]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1} 
[Inverse modeling techniques]{.badge .bg-secondary .mb-1}

[📚 View Relevant Publications](publications.html?search=Others){.btn .btn-sm .btn-primary .mt-3}
:::
:::
